entries.shortDescription: Other usable lane sessions exist for the actor and may be exported or adopted.
commonCauses: The same actor minted more than one active lane.
A previous lane was left open but remains within TTL.
remediation: Continue with the current lane when the duplicate is expected.
Export one of the listed ATM_LANE_SESSION_ID values when resuming that lane.
Run node atm.mjs lane sweep --json to inspect stale lane cleanup candidates.

entries.shortDescription: A closed lane session cannot accept a heartbeat.
commonCauses: The lane was already released or expired.
A stale ATM_LANE_SESSION_ID points at a closed runtime lane.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs lane status --actor <actor> --json to mint or resolve a usable lane.
Do not manually edit .atm/runtime/lane-sessions; use lane status, adopt, or sweep.

entries.shortDescription: A TTL-expired lane session cannot be revived by heartbeat.
commonCauses: The lane exceeded its TTL before the heartbeat command ran.
The operator attempted to reuse an old lane instead of minting or adopting a fresh one.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs lane status --actor <actor> --json to mint a fresh lane.
Run node atm.mjs lane sweep --write --json only when explicit cleanup evidence is desired.

entries.shortDescription: A lane session heartbeat was recorded and the lane TTL was extended.
commonCauses: An agent or tool intentionally refreshed a live lane session.
remediation: No remediation is required; inspect evidence.eventPath for the heartbeat event when auditing.

entries.shortDescription: Heartbeat requires an explicit lane id or ATM_LANE_SESSION_ID.
commonCauses: The command was run as lane heartbeat without a positional lane id.
ATM_LANE_SESSION_ID was not exported in the environment.
remediation: Pass node atm.mjs lane heartbeat <lane-id> --json.
Run node atm.mjs lane status --json first and export the reported ATM_LANE_SESSION_ID.

entries.shortDescription: A new lane session was lazily minted for the current actor.
commonCauses: No lane id was provided and ATM_LANE_SESSION_ID was unset.
The requested lane was missing, closed, or expired.
remediation: Export the reported ATM_LANE_SESSION_ID when subsequent commands should stay on the same lane.
Use node atm.mjs lane heartbeat <lane-id> --json to refresh long-running lanes.

entries.shortDescription: The requested lane session is closed and cannot be adopted.
commonCauses: The lane status is released or expired.
A stale handoff command referenced an old lane id.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs lane status --actor <actor> --json to mint or resolve a current lane.
Use lane sweep only for command-backed cleanup of expired lanes.

entries.shortDescription: The requested lane session id does not exist in the runtime lane store.
commonCauses: ATM_LANE_SESSION_ID points to a lane from another repository or cleaned runtime.
The lane id was mistyped.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs lane status --actor <actor> --json to resolve or mint a usable lane.
Check the reported exportHint before retrying adopt or heartbeat.

entries.shortDescription: The requested lane environment id is missing, closed, or expired, so ATM minted a new lane.
commonCauses: ATM_LANE_SESSION_ID was copied from an old shell.
The referenced lane expired before the next command.
remediation: Export the new lane id from the command output.
Run node atm.mjs lane sweep --json to inspect stale lane candidates.

entries.shortDescription: Lane sweep expired sweepable TTL-expired lane sessions and wrote sweep events.
commonCauses: The operator ran lane sweep with --write after reviewing stale lane candidates.
remediation: No remediation is required; inspect evidence.sweep and eventPaths for the cleanup record.

entries.shortDescription: Lane sweep reported stale lane candidates without mutating runtime state.
commonCauses: The operator ran lane sweep without --write.
An agent collected lane cleanup evidence before deciding whether to expire stale lanes.
remediation: If the report is acceptable, rerun with --write to expire sweepable lanes.
Leave the lanes untouched when another actor may still own the session.

entries.shortDescription: Runner-sync steward queue head was released after receipt-backed build/publication evidence.
commonCauses: A queue-head runner-sync build completed and produced a receipt reference or digest.
remediation: No remediation is required; inspect evidence.release.next for the next steward work item, if any.

entries.shortDescription: Another governed commit is currently finalizing the same branch.
commonCauses: A concurrent captain or worker is inside the serialized branch commit queue.
A stale branch queue lock exists after an interrupted commit attempt.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs git commit-status --actor <actor> --json to inspect the branchCommitQueueStatus owner and age.
If the owner is alive, wait for it to finish and retry the same governed commit command.
If the owner is stale or dead, inspect the lock path and current HEAD before cleaning it through the governed recovery lane.

entries.shortDescription: A Team Broker ownership conflict blocks commit bypass until a recorded conflict-resolution artifact and approval exist.
commonCauses: A commit would bypass an active Team Broker cross-task mutation block.
A --no-verify or broker override path was attempted without broker-conflict resolution evidence.
remediation: Resolve the ownership conflict through a paper-style broker conflict-resolution artifact.
Obtain the required backend.brokerConflictOverride approval only when a human has approved the override.
Retry the governed commit with --broker-conflict-resolution and the approved lease.

entries.shortDescription: A git add helper using stdin pathspecs is active and can make a governed commit appear hung.
commonCauses: A GUI/editor staging helper launched git add --pathspec-from-file=- and left stdin open.
A previous scripted staging command is still waiting for input instead of receiving a finite path list.
The operator retried commit while a helper process was still active.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs git commit-status --actor <actor> --json and inspect stdinPathspecGitAddProcesses.
Wait briefly for the listed helper to finish, or terminate only the listed stuck git add process after confirming it is the stdin pathspec helper.
Manual staging with git add -- <paths> is normal and safe when the path list is finite and reviewed.
For governed scoped delivery, prefer node atm.mjs git commit --auto-stage; ATM auto-stage uses explicit git add -- <paths> and does not use stdin pathspec staging.

entries.shortDescription: No active stdin pathspec git add helper is currently visible to ATM commit-status.
commonCauses: The commit-status diagnostic found no git add --pathspec-from-file=- process.
Any previous UI/helper staging process has exited.
remediation: Proceed with explicit staging or the governed ATM auto-stage lane.
If the UI still appears stuck, inspect the branch commit queue and recent commit attempt status before retrying.

entries.shortDescription: ATM could not match the prompt to an imported task card or ledger task.
commonCauses: The task exists only in the planning repository and has not been imported.
The prompt mentions a task family or hint that does not match a target-repo ledger record.
remediation: If authoring a new task, create or update the planning card first.
Dry-run import the planning card with node atm.mjs tasks import --from <card> --dry-run --json.
When the dry run is clean, import with --write and commit the ledger import bundle before claiming implementation.

entries.shortDescription: ATM refused a frozen-runner write because the stable runner is older than source changes.
commonCauses: Source files changed after release/atm-onefile/atm.mjs was generated.
A write command such as task import, claim, close, or commit tried to use node atm.mjs while runnerMode.sourceDrift.syncRequired is true.
Another active captain owns the release/generated or release mirror surface, so rebuilding immediately would risk absorbing foreign work.
remediation: If no other captain owns release surfaces, run ATM_RETAIN_RELEASE_ARTIFACTS=1 npm run build and retry the frozen node atm.mjs write command.
If another captain owns release/generated or release mirrors, enqueue broker runner-sync work for the sealed source SHA and wait for the steward release receipt.
For read-only source-first validation only, use node atm.dev.mjs; do not use source-first writes to bypass frozen-runner governance.
relatedCommands: node atm.mjs broker runner-sync enqueue --task <task-id> --actor <actor> --sealed-source-sha <sha> --surface release/atm-onefile/atm.mjs --json

entries.shortDescription: The frozen ATM runner is older than source changes and must be rebuilt before frozen-runner validation is trusted.
commonCauses: Source files changed after release/atm-onefile/atm.mjs was generated.
An agent validated with node atm.dev.mjs but did not refresh the frozen runner used by node atm.mjs.
remediation: Run ATM_RETAIN_RELEASE_ARTIFACTS=1 npm run build from the framework repository.
Rerun the same node atm.mjs command after the build and confirm runnerMode.sourceDrift.syncRequired is false.

commonCauses: A runner-sync steward release was attempted with an invalid queue state or missing publication receipt.
A captain tried to release a runner-sync build that is not the current queue head or is not waiting on that steward work id.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs broker runner-sync status --task <task-id> --json to inspect stewardWorkId, queuePosition, waitingTasks, and suggestedNextAction.
Only the queue-head steward work may be released after one build has produced a receipt reference or digest.
Retry release with node atm.mjs broker runner-sync release --task <task-id> --steward-work-id <id> --receipt-ref <path> --json, or wait for the current owner to release first.
entries.shortDescription: Runner-sync steward release failed before ATM could classify a more specific release error.

commonCauses: A runner-sync steward release was attempted with an invalid queue state or missing publication receipt.
A captain tried to release a runner-sync build that is not the current queue head or is not waiting on that steward work id.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs broker runner-sync status --task <task-id> --json to inspect stewardWorkId, queuePosition, waitingTasks, and suggestedNextAction.
Only the queue-head steward work may be released after one build has produced a receipt reference or digest.
Retry release with node atm.mjs broker runner-sync release --task <task-id> --steward-work-id <id> --receipt-ref <path> --json, or wait for the current owner to release first.
entries.shortDescription: Runner-sync steward release was invoked without a task id or steward work id.

commonCauses: A runner-sync steward release was attempted with an invalid queue state or missing publication receipt.
A captain tried to release a runner-sync build that is not the current queue head or is not waiting on that steward work id.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs broker runner-sync status --task <task-id> --json to inspect stewardWorkId, queuePosition, waitingTasks, and suggestedNextAction.
Only the queue-head steward work may be released after one build has produced a receipt reference or digest.
Retry release with node atm.mjs broker runner-sync release --task <task-id> --steward-work-id <id> --receipt-ref <path> --json, or wait for the current owner to release first.
entries.shortDescription: The requested runner-sync steward work id is not present in the steward queue.

commonCauses: A runner-sync steward release was attempted with an invalid queue state or missing publication receipt.
A captain tried to release a runner-sync build that is not the current queue head or is not waiting on that steward work id.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs broker runner-sync status --task <task-id> --json to inspect stewardWorkId, queuePosition, waitingTasks, and suggestedNextAction.
Only the queue-head steward work may be released after one build has produced a receipt reference or digest.
Retry release with node atm.mjs broker runner-sync release --task <task-id> --steward-work-id <id> --receipt-ref <path> --json, or wait for the current owner to release first.
entries.shortDescription: The requested runner-sync steward work is waiting behind another sealed source and cannot be released yet.

commonCauses: A runner-sync steward release was attempted with an invalid queue state or missing publication receipt.
A captain tried to release a runner-sync build that is not the current queue head or is not waiting on that steward work id.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs broker runner-sync status --task <task-id> --json to inspect stewardWorkId, queuePosition, waitingTasks, and suggestedNextAction.
Only the queue-head steward work may be released after one build has produced a receipt reference or digest.
Retry release with node atm.mjs broker runner-sync release --task <task-id> --steward-work-id <id> --receipt-ref <path> --json, or wait for the current owner to release first.
entries.shortDescription: The releasing task is not one of the tasks waiting on the requested runner-sync steward work id.

commonCauses: A runner-sync steward release was attempted with an invalid queue state or missing publication receipt.
A captain tried to release a runner-sync build that is not the current queue head or is not waiting on that steward work id.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs broker runner-sync status --task <task-id> --json to inspect stewardWorkId, queuePosition, waitingTasks, and suggestedNextAction.
Only the queue-head steward work may be released after one build has produced a receipt reference or digest.
Retry release with node atm.mjs broker runner-sync release --task <task-id> --steward-work-id <id> --receipt-ref <path> --json, or wait for the current owner to release first.
entries.shortDescription: Runner-sync steward release requires a build/publication receipt reference or digest before the queue head can be cleared.
Task audit passed but found active warnings that should be reviewed before claiming a clean ledger.
commonCauses: Planning-only done records have not been imported as target-repo closure evidence.
Static evidence exists without command-backed execution metadata.
remediation: Read activeFindings in the tasks audit JSON output.
Do not treat advisory output as a blocker unless the active task depends on the warned evidence.
Open a repair or import follow-up when the warning is in the current delivery path.

entries.shortDescription: A Team command that needs a task binding was invoked without --task <id>.
commonCauses: The operator ran team plan, start, validate, patrol, or another task-scoped Team command without --task.
A copied Team command omitted the task id while retrying from a previous diagnostic.
remediation: Rerun the command with --task <task-id> and the same actor/recipe options.
Use node atm.mjs next --prompt "<current goal>" --json when unsure which task should own the Team command.
Do not infer a task from dirty files; bind the Team command to the current ATM task ledger entry.

prefixRules.shortDescription: Git governance, staging, commit, branch queue, or push admission reported a guarded condition.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs git commit-status --actor <actor> --json when the condition happened during commit or staging.
Run node atm.mjs git admit --actor <actor> --branch <branch> --remote origin --json before protected push retries.
Do not bypass hooks or broker conflicts unless an explicit emergency approval names the required backend permission.

prefixRules.shortDescription: Taskflow refused or diagnosed a task open, pre-close, close, delivery, or evidence transition.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs taskflow pre-close --task <task-id> --actor <actor> --json for close-readiness details.
Inspect excludedDirtyFiles, missing validators, and commit-bundle diagnostics before retrying close.
Use taskflow close only after real deliverables and validator evidence exist.

prefixRules.shortDescription: Task ledger, task scope, claim, audit, or lifecycle governance reported a guarded condition.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs tasks status --task <task-id> --json for the current ledger and claim state.
Use node atm.mjs next --prompt "<current goal>" --json when the correct task route is unclear.
Do not hand-edit .atm/history task records; use the ATM task lifecycle command named by the diagnostic.

prefixRules.shortDescription: Team Agents planning, start, validation, runtime, knowledge, or roster governance reported a guarded condition.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs team status --compact --json to inspect active Team runs.
Rerun the relevant team plan or validate command with the same --task and --recipe values.
Coordinator remains the task lifecycle and git.write owner unless Team start --execute explicitly granted a governed execution lane.

prefixRules.shortDescription: Frozen-runner freshness, runner-sync, or release publication governance reported a guarded condition.
remediation: Read runnerMode.sourceDrift in the command output before retrying writes.
If syncRequired is true and you own release surfaces, run ATM_RETAIN_RELEASE_ARTIFACTS=1 npm run build.
If another actor owns release surfaces, use broker runner-sync enqueue/status and wait for the steward receipt.

prefixRules.shortDescription: Team Broker, shared surface, steward, or conflict admission reported a guarded condition.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs broker status --json to inspect current shared-surface ownership.
Do not bypass broker-conflict-blocked results; obtain a conflict-resolution artifact and required approval when the diagnostic asks for one.
Prefer waiting, steward release, or documented takeover over ad hoc staging or direct runtime edits.

prefixRules.shortDescription: Prompt-scoped routing, claim admission, or next-action guidance reported a guarded condition.
remediation: Rerun node atm.mjs next --prompt "<current goal>" --json and read evidence.nextAction before editing.
If the prompt refers to a planning card, import or identify the target-repo task before claim.
If active work exists, inspect broker and task status before widening scope.

prefixRules.shortDescription: A local ATM guard found a policy, encoding, framework-development, or protected-state condition.
remediation: Read the guard report findings and repair only the listed files or state.
Run the same guard command again after the focused repair.
Do not bypass guard findings unless a documented emergency approval explicitly allows that protected action.

prefixRules.shortDescription: An ATM validation command found a failing invariant, stale projection, missing evidence, or governance mismatch.
remediation: Read the validator output and rerun the same command after the focused repair.
If the validator names a generated projection, run the paired --write or generator command first.
Do not treat validation output as task evidence until the command exits successfully.

prefixRules.shortDescription: The ATM CLI parser or command dispatcher rejected the command shape.
remediation: Run node atm.mjs <command> --help for the supported options and examples.
Retry with explicit --json, --actor, --task, or required positional arguments named by the diagnostic.
Do not infer missing task or actor identity from dirty files.

prefixRules.shortDescription: ATM reported a guarded condition that does not yet have a narrower exact entry or prefix rule.
remediation: Read the command's JSON details and messages before retrying.
Run npm run generate:error-codes to refresh the source index and locate the owning source context.
Add an exact registry entry when the code is user-visible or appears in recurring operator reports.
